# CSS Un-Center Pro

> Layout Destruction Suite -- Making divs suffer since 2026.

CSS Un-Center Pro is a humorous interactive React web app that demonstrates destructive CSS anti-patterns. Users apply CSS snippets to a live preview and watch the layout progressively break. It features a retro terminal aesthetic with glitch effects and scanlines.

## Tech stack

- React 18 with TypeScript
- Vite 5 (build tool and dev server)
- Tailwind CSS 3
- Lucide React (icons)
- No backend; purely client-side

## Repository structure

- src/App.tsx: Main component, manages chaos state and animations
- src/constants.ts: CSS snippets, chaos tips, glitch characters
- src/utils.ts: glitchText() utility
- src/index.css: Tailwind directives and global styles
- src/components/Header.tsx: Title and badge tags
- src/components/ChaosMeter.tsx: Chaos level progress bar
- src/components/LivePreview.tsx: Interactive preview that deteriorates with chaos
- src/components/SnippetPanel.tsx: Buttons to apply CSS snippets
- src/components/Footer.tsx: Misaligned footer
- src/components/Overlays.tsx: Scanline and vignette visual effects
- src/hooks/useGlitchTitle.ts: Periodic title glitch effect
- src/hooks/useRotatingTip.ts: Rotating pro-tip display

## Key concepts

- Chaos level: 0-100%, increases by 25% per applied snippet (4 snippets total)
- CSS snippets: Defined in constants.ts with id, label, target, and CSS code
- Components receive chaos level as a prop and conditionally style based on thresholds
- Dynamic styling uses inline styles; static styling uses Tailwind classes
- Color palette: #00ffff (cyan primary), #000 (background), #ff9900 (warning), #ff0000 (danger)

## Commands

- npm run dev: Start dev server (port 5173)
- npm run build: Production build to dist/
- npm run lint: ESLint
- npm run typecheck: TypeScript type checking
- npm run preview: Preview production build
